description: Skill Creation Workflow (Skill Factory) - Create kernel/skill_factory.py that can programmatically create new skills (directory + SKILL.md + register in _index.yaml), wire into EvolutionEngine's add_skill handler, add validation for kebab-case names.
steps: Create kernel/skill_factory.py with class SkillFactory(knowledge_dir: str): method create_skill(name: str, description: str, content: str, tags: list[str]) -> Path that: validates name is kebab-case (a-z0-9 with hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphens), creates knowledge/skills/{name}/ directory, writes SKILL.md with the content (header: # {name}, description paragraph, then content), calls KnowledgeStore.add_skill() to register in _index.yaml, returns the path to the created skill directory

Add method create_skill_from_template(name: str, description: str, sections: dict[str, str], tags: list[str]) -> Path that generates SKILL.md from named sections (e.g. {'overview': '...', 'usage': '...'}) following the write-a-skill format
Add validation: raise ValueError for invalid names (not kebab-case, empty, has uppercase, starts/ends with hyphen). Add validate_skill_name(name) -> tuple[bool, str] helper.

Wire into EvolutionEngine.apply_change(): when change_type == 'add_skill', instead of just passing/logging, call SkillFactory.create_skill() using details from the change dict (details should have: name, description, content, tags)

Add tests in tests/test_skill_factory.py: test create_skill creates directory and SKILL.md, test name validation rejects invalid names (uppercase, spaces, leading hyphens), test create_skill_from_template generates proper format, test that _index.yaml is updated after creation, test EvolutionEngine integration with add_skill type
acceptance_criteria: create_skill() creates directory + SKILL.md + registers in index
EvolutionEngine's add_skill handler calls SkillFactory instead of just logging
findings: All 850 tests pass with 98.88% coverage. SkillFactory at 100% coverage. EvolutionEngine add_skill now calls SkillFactory.create_skill(). Invalid names cause ValueError which is caught by the engine's except clause and logged as failed.
